steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure. Read both sentences: the first makes a broad claim about high-speed rail expanding in many countries; the second introduces Denmark as a specific case, signaled by 'for instance.' The structure is general claim followed by concrete example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The text rejects a long-held belief and then proposes a different interpretation': nothing is rejected; the first sentence is a straightforward claim, not a belief being overturned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The text questions a course of action and then suggests alternatives to that action': the text does not question anything or propose alternatives — it simply states a trend and illustrates it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The text summarizes an argument and then offers a challenge to that argument': the Denmark sentence supports the first sentence rather than challenging it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The text notes a general trend and then cites an example of that trend': sentence one states the general trend (high-speed rail expanding in many countries); sentence two cites Denmark as a specific example, introduced by 'for instance.' ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
